title: antirrhinum majus var. angustifolium
intro: Antirrhinum majus var. angustifolium, also known as the narrow-leaved snapdragon, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Plantaginaceae family and is native to Europe. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to improve respiratory function, reduce inflammation, and act as an antispasmodic, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Antirrhinum majus var. angustifolium can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the skin for therapeutic purposes.
